description: Customer says yes immediately — agent should send checkout link without re-pitching. The opener's 5% call-completion offer applies (the customer accepted the close, which carried the 5%).

turns.customer: yeah I want to buy the chair, send me the link

description: Customer raises price; agent isolates; customer confirms; agent should escalate (reframe / alt / discount) — NOT reset to discovery.

turns.customer: i like the chair but it's a bit out of my budget
yes that would be the only thing

description: Customer asks about quality; agent should call get_review_summary and quote real reviews.

turns.customer: hi, i'm not sure about this chair
what do other people actually say about it?

description: Customer asks how many are left; agent should call check_inventory and cite real number.

turns.customer: how many of these do you actually have left?

description: Customer asks when they'd get it; agent should call get_delivery_eta.

turns.customer: if I order today how soon would it arrive at zip 90210?

description: VIP customer with 4 prior orders — agent should reference the relationship at hello, not generic.
Lapsed customer (last order 280 days ago) — agent should acknowledge the gap softly, not pretend it's a fresh visit.
First-time customer — agent should NOT pretend to know them.
expected_text_not_contains_any: nice to hear from you again

description: Customer says they're not interested after several turns; agent should send product info on WhatsApp and end gracefully — not push a checkout.

turns.customer: actually i'm not interested in the chair anymore
no, i don't want it. please don't send a discount or anything

description: Two PRICE objections — agent should be willing to offer a real discount (5%) on the third turn.
Customer opens with a vague hi — agent should ASK before pitching, not lead with features.
When asked vaguely about availability, the agent must NOT invent fake scarcity — either call check_inventory or say it doesn't know.

turns.customer: is this going to sell out soon?

description: When asked 'is this popular', agent must call get_recent_purchases or say it doesn't have that data — must NOT invent numbers.
Customer says they're just browsing on turn 2 — agent should treat as soft no, ask ONE diagnostic question, NOT immediately back off.

turns.customer: honestly i'm just browsing, not really looking to buy

description: Customer rejects 3 times — agent pushes diagnostic + value + clean ask, then on the 4th turn finally fires product_info graceful exit.

turns.customer: honestly i'm just browsing, not really looking to buy

description: Customer says they already bought one — out-of-fit signal, agent should NOT push, exit immediately with grace.

turns.customer: i already ordered the same chair from a different site yesterday

description: VIP raises price twice — should get the discount sooner than a stranger would (relationship discount).
First turn — agent must introduce itself by name + business, name cart items, surface the 5% call-completion offer, and ask for the close.
Opener should reference at least one cart item BY NAME, not generic.
Customer says 'no' — agent should ask one diagnostic question about the concern, not jump to graceful exit.
Customer explicitly says 'don't call me again' — agent must NOT keep probing; should exit gracefully (and ideally NOT send any tool).

turns.customer: please don't call me again, take me off your list

description: Lapsed customer (last order 280 days ago) — opener should naturally acknowledge it's been a while.
